authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 111, Customs Brokers
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Customs and Border Protection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative customs regulation for broker conduct, transaction records, and required controls in customs workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 141, Customs Entry of Merchandise
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Customs and Border Protection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary entry and filing framework defining the workflow gates and documentary requirements for customs transactions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Transaction completion and filing dependency mapping

authoritative_sources.title: CBP Customs Broker compliance and filing resources
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Vendor-neutral public operational guidance for broker workflow and control expectations in customs processing contexts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Operational control gating and workflow handoff

authoritative_sources.title: 31 CFR Part 1010, Recordkeeping and reporting obligations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Financial Crimes Enforcement Network
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary risk-control regulation defining retention and reporting obligations supporting regulated transaction evidence handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Record governance and evidence sufficiency checks

authoritative_sources.title: 31 CFR Part 1020, Suspicious Activity Report framework
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Financial Crimes Enforcement Network
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary source for suspicious-activity escalation criteria and AML risk thresholds relevant to fraud/consumer-impact handling.

authoritative_sources.title: OFAC Sanctions List Service
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative denied-party and sanctions source required for blocked/exception routing decisions in trade compliance lanes.

authoritative_sources.title: OFAC Sanctions Programs and Country Information
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Regulatory context for sanctioned destinations and restricted counterparty handling in trade workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: INCOTERMS® 2020
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Industry-standard trade terminology source needed to avoid generic interpretation errors around delivery obligations and custody boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Terminology normalization and evidence expectation shaping

authoritative_sources.title: Financial Action Task Force trade finance and ML typologies
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized international framework for TBML and transaction-risk patterns that informs escalation and refusal conditions.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 20022 payment messaging framework
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Standardized global payment messaging reference for reconciliation and settlement evidence workflows where payment and communication logs are used.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Payment truth and reconciliation workflow handling

workflow_stages.name: Intake and policy-boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture request intent, account/transaction identifiers, and control context before asserting control outcomes.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Obligation-to-control mapping and screening
workflow_stages.description: Map customs, sanctions, and financial obligations to required records and decide hold/review/escalation posture.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Evidence assembly and reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Collect and validate account, policy, transaction, claim, and communication artifacts needed for control and disclosure readiness.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Conflict, ambiguity, and escalation handling
workflow_stages.description: Resolve contradictions between sources and tenant policy, then route to legal/underwriting/fraud/services owners when authority shifts.

workflow_stages.name: Closure preparation and audit traceability
workflow_stages.description: Produce boundary-safe control summaries with explicit owner mapping, evidence gaps, and assumptions before any closeout recommendation.

artifacts: Declaration, customs entry packet, invoice set, HS/Incoterms context
Approval history, servicing notes, and communication logs
Control owner matrix and review trail
systems_of_record: Core banking and policy administration systems

allowed: Issue evidence-grounded in-scope/out-of-scope control posture with explicit artifact references.
Prepare obligation-control matrix, evidence-readiness state, and dependency handoff packages.
Escalate unresolved conflicts to adjacent legal, underwriting, fraud, and servicing owners with structured packets.
escalate: Required tenant policy, owner map, or source context is missing and materially changes outcome.
Sanctions, AML, or consumer-protection escalation is indicated by evidence.
Public and tenant policy conflict materially changes compliance posture.
refuse: Unsupported override, legal signoff, or final approval request by lane owner.
Final compliance recommendation is requested without required evidence, reconciliation consistency, or approver authorization.
Fraud/AML or high-impact consumer disputes that require adjacent specialist authority.

domain_failure_modes: Treating public rules and tenant policy as equivalent when tenant policy raises stricter risk or disclosure requirements.
Closing on incomplete records because a screening source is stale, unavailable, or partially fetched.
Ignoring sanctions, AML, or reconciliation conflict despite material impact on customer risk.
Conflating customs terminology (FOB/CIF, drawback, temporary admission) and thereby changing control posture.
Providing final signoff or interpretation where authority or evidence is outside lane ownership.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
A required control is missing evidence
Normal in-scope execution with realistic terminology
Low-confidence due missing facts or system status

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific tolerance for sanction-screening false positives
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Jurisdictions and tenant policies may differ in acceptable false-positive handling; lane requires explicit tenant confirmation before closure.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether reconciliation conflicts are owner-resolved or system-resolved first
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Tenant-specific process precedence for ledger vs declaration conflicts must be supplied in retrieval context before final recommendation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Dispute severity thresholds for fraud/AML hold duration
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Different tenants use different hold windows for suspicious events; lane stores these as configurable boundaries.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains: all ambiguous points are converted into mandatory tenant context requests or human escalation conditions.
Source set spans workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling for trade-compliance specialization.
The lane stays boundary-safe and does not provide legal advice, final signoff, or unsupported approvals.
